name: Gunnar
description: Gunnar was a legendary Norse hero, a king of Burgundy from the Völsunga saga and the Nibelungenlied. He is best known for his role in the tragic tale of Sigurd (Siegfried) and Brynhild. Gunnar, along with his brothers Hogni and Guttorm, and his sister Gudrun, were central figures in the downfall of the Burgundians. Gunnar was renowned for his strength and chivalry, particularly his unmatched ability to play the harp, even with his hands bound. He desired Brynhild as his wife, but she was destined for the strongest man, Sigurd. Through trickery and deception, Sigurd helped Gunnar win Brynhild, leading to a complex web of love, betrayal, and ultimately, death. Gunnar swore an oath of brotherhood with Sigurd, which he later broke due to Brynhild's machinations and his own ambition. He participated in Sigurd's murder, a decision that haunted him and ultimately led to his own demise. Gunnar's story is a cautionary tale about the destructive power of jealousy, betrayal, and the consequences of breaking sacred oaths. His unwavering loyalty to his brothers, even in the face of overwhelming adversity, is also a testament to the importance of kinship in Norse society. He died heroically, refusing to reveal the location of the hoard of gold, even under torture. His tale highlights themes of fate, free will, and the tragic flaws that can lead to the downfall of even the noblest of heroes. Gunnar's legend persists as a significant element of Norse mythology, demonstrating the complexity and depth of its characters and narratives. The Nibelungenlied presents a slightly different, but still tragic, account of his life and demise.
subtitle: King of Burgundy, Betrayed by Love
achievements: Maintaining his oath of loyalty to his brothers
Ruling Burgundy with strength and authority
His bravery in the face of torture

keyMyths.title: The Wooing of Brynhild
keyMyths.description: Gunnar desires Brynhild but cannot overcome the trials to win her. Sigurd, using the cloak of invisibility and Gunnar's form, completes the trials for him, leading to Brynhild marrying Gunnar. This deception sets the stage for future tragedy.

keyMyths.title: The Murder of Sigurd
keyMyths.description: Driven by Brynhild's resentment and jealousy, Gunnar conspires with his brothers to murder Sigurd. He cannot bring himself to kill Sigurd directly, so he tasks Guttorm with the deed. This act breaks the oath between Gunnar and Sigurd.

keyMyths.title: Gunnar's Death
keyMyths.description: Gunnar is eventually captured by Atli (Attila the Hun), who seeks the hoard of gold. Gunnar refuses to reveal its location, even under torture, and is thrown into a snake pit, where he dies playing the harp with his toes.
